report_name: VRChat Cloud Breach Exposes Data of 2.4 Million Users, Including Login History and Linked Accounts

threat_actors.name: Unauthorized VRChat Cloud Intruder
threat_actors.description: The unidentified third party that gained unauthorized access to VRChat's cloud environment between May 10 and May 12, 2026, and exfiltrated data of 2.4 million users.
threat_actors.goals: Exfiltrate user data for potential misuse such as targeted phishing campaigns and credential stuffing attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attacker likely obtained and used legitimate credentials for VRChat's cloud environment to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Once inside the cloud environment, the attacker likely performed reconnaissance to identify where valuable user data was stored.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The attacker exfiltrated user data from cloud storage objects, such as storage buckets or databases.

identities.name: VRChat Inc.
identities.description: The social VR platform that suffered a data breach affecting 2,436,782 users due to unauthorized access to its cloud environment.

identities.name: Steam
identities.description: A gaming platform whose user IDs, when linked to VRChat accounts, were exposed in the data breach.

identities.name: Meta
identities.description: A social media and technology company whose user IDs, when linked to VRChat accounts, were exposed in the data breach.

indicators.name: Cloud Storage Data Exfiltration via CLI
indicators.pattern:
title: Cloud Storage Data Exfiltration via CLI
id: 2b9c1a8f-3d4e-4f5a-8b6c-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of cloud command-line interface (CLI) tools to download data from storage services, which could be indicative of data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/data-breaches/2026/06/data-of-2-4-million-vrchat-users-stolen
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection_aws:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\aws.exe'
      - '/aws'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 's3'
      - 'cp'
  selection_azure:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\az.exe'
      - '/az'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'storage'
      - 'blob'
      - 'download'
  condition: 1 of them
indicators.description: Detects the use of cloud CLI tools (like AWS CLI or Azure CLI) to download data from storage, a technique potentially used to exfiltrate the VRChat user data.

relationships.source_name: Cloud Storage Data Exfiltration via CLI
